Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class NullCard

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Represents an empty expansion slot in the Apple IIe, providing no functionality.

public class NullCard : ICard, IConfigurable
Inheritance
object
NullCard
Implements
ICard
IConfigurable
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

The NullCard is a special implementation of ICard that represents the absence of a peripheral card. It is the only card type guaranteed to be available in the emulator and serves as the default state for all expansion slots upon initialization.

Purpose and Design:

  • Default State: All slots (1-7) are initialized with NullCard instances, representing empty slots
  • Removal Placeholder: When a card is removed via RemoveCard(SlotNumber), it's replaced with a NullCard
  • Floating Bus Enabler: By returning null for all read operations, NullCard causes the slots system to return floating bus values
  • Silent Writes: All write operations are no-ops, preventing any state changes or side effects

Behavioral Characteristics:
The NullCard implements complete passivity:

  • All read methods return null (indicating "no response")
  • All write methods perform no operation
  • No internal state is maintained (completely stateless)
  • Cloning creates a new instance (though functionally identical)
  • Configuration methods are no-ops (GetMetadata() returns empty, ApplyMetadata(string) always succeeds)

Configuration Support:
NullCard implements IConfigurable (via ICard) with trivial behavior: GetMetadata() always returns an empty string, and ApplyMetadata(string) always returns true while ignoring any provided metadata. This allows NullCard to participate in configuration serialization without requiring special-case handling.

Real Hardware Analogy:
The NullCard emulates the behavior of an actual Apple IIe with no card installed in a slot. When software accesses an empty slot's I/O or ROM space, the hardware returns whatever data happens to be floating on the bus at that moment, which this implementation delegates to the IFloatingBusProvider.

Factory Integration:
The ICardFactory must always be able to provide NullCard instances via GetNullCard(). This is enforced during Slots initialization and card removal operations, which throw exceptions if NullCard creation fails.

Thread Safety:
The NullCard is completely stateless and therefore inherently thread-safe. Multiple threads can safely call any methods simultaneously without synchronization, though normal emulator usage restricts all card operations to the emulator worker thread.

Properties

Description

Gets a brief description of the NullCard.

public string Description { get; }

Property Value

string

Always returns "No card".

Remarks

This minimal description is appropriate since the NullCard represents the absence of functionality rather than any actual peripheral device.

Id

Gets the unique identifier for the NullCard type.

public int Id { get; }

Property Value

int

Always returns 0.

Remarks

The ID value of 0 is reserved for NullCard and serves as a sentinel value throughout the emulator. Code can check card.Id == 0 to determine if a slot is empty, which is formalized in the IsEmpty(SlotNumber) convenience method.

All other card types must use IDs ≥ 1 to avoid conflicts. The ICardFactory validates that no registered card uses ID 0 during registration.

Name

Gets the display name for empty slots.

public string Name { get; }

Property Value

string

Always returns "Empty Slot".

Remarks

This name appears in UI elements like slot configuration dialogs and status displays to indicate that no peripheral card is installed.

Methods

ApplyMetadata(string)

Applies configuration metadata to this NullCard.

public bool ApplyMetadata(string metadata)

Parameters

metadata string

The configuration metadata (ignored).

Returns

bool

Always returns true since NullCard accepts any metadata without effect.

Remarks

NullCard is completely stateless and cannot be configured. This method ignores the provided metadata and always returns true to indicate "success" in the sense that the NullCard remains in its expected state (no configuration).

This implementation allows NullCard to participate in configuration restoration without requiring special-case handling. Empty slots are simply skipped during configuration, and any metadata provided is silently ignored.

Clone()

Creates a new instance of NullCard.

public ICard Clone()

Returns

ICard

A new NullCard instance.

Remarks

Although NullCard is stateless and all instances are functionally identical, the Clone() method creates a new instance to maintain consistency with the ICard contract and allow each slot to have its own card reference.

This is used during Slots initialization to populate all slots with independent NullCard instances, even though they behave identically.

GetMetadata()

Gets the configuration metadata for this NullCard.

public string GetMetadata()

Returns

string

Always returns an empty string since NullCard has no configurable state.

Remarks

NullCard is completely stateless and has no configuration to persist. This method always returns an empty string to indicate the absence of any configuration data.

This implementation satisfies the IConfigurable contract inherited through ICard, allowing NullCard to participate in configuration serialization without requiring special-case handling.

ReadExtendedRom(ushort)

Attempts to read from the card's extended ROM space, always returning null.

public byte? ReadExtendedRom(ushort _)

Parameters

_ ushort

The extended ROM offset (ignored).

Returns

byte?

Always null, indicating no response.

Remarks

The null return causes ISlots to return a floating bus value. Even when an empty slot's extended ROM is "selected" via BankSelect, reads return floating bus values since no physical ROM is present.

ReadIO(byte)

Attempts to read from the card's I/O space, always returning null.

public byte? ReadIO(byte _)

Parameters

_ byte

The I/O offset (ignored).

Returns

byte?

Always null, indicating no response.

Remarks

The null return causes ISlots to return a floating bus value, emulating the behavior of reading from an empty slot on real Apple IIe hardware.

ReadRom(byte)

Attempts to read from the card's ROM space, always returning null.

public byte? ReadRom(byte _)

Parameters

_ byte

The ROM offset (ignored).

Returns

byte?

Always null, indicating no response.

Remarks

The null return causes ISlots to return a floating bus value. On real hardware, reading from an empty slot's ROM space returns whatever data happens to be on the bus.

WriteExtendedRom(ushort, byte)

Attempts to write to the card's extended ROM space, performing no operation.

public void WriteExtendedRom(ushort _, byte _1)

Parameters

_ ushort

The extended ROM offset (ignored).

_1 byte

The value to write (ignored).

Remarks

Writes to empty slot extended ROM space are silently ignored, matching real hardware behavior.

WriteIO(byte, byte)

Attempts to write to the card's I/O space, performing no operation.

public void WriteIO(byte _, byte _1)

Parameters

_ byte

The I/O offset (ignored).

_1 byte

The value to write (ignored).

Remarks

Writes to empty slots are silently ignored, matching real hardware behavior where writes to unpopulated slots have no effect.

WriteRom(byte, byte)

Attempts to write to the card's ROM space, performing no operation.

public void WriteRom(byte _, byte _1)

Parameters

_ byte

The ROM offset (ignored).

_1 byte

The value to write (ignored).

Remarks

Writes to empty slot ROM space are silently ignored, matching real hardware behavior.
